 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-542","title":"Create acfs session list command","description":"# Task: Create acfs session list command

## Context
Part of EPIC: Agent Session Sharing and Replay (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0sb)

## Rationale
Before export, users need to see what sessions are available.

## Command
```bash
acfs session list
# Output:
# Recent Sessions (last 30 days):
#   abc123  2025-01-15 10:30  claude-code  45m  "Implemented auth..."
#   def456  2025-01-14 14:00  codex        20m  "Fixed pagination bug"
#   ghi789  2025-01-13 09:15  claude-code  60m  "Refactored API..."
#
# Use: acfs session export --session-id <id> to export

acfs session list --json
# JSON array of sessions
```

## Implementation
- Call CASS under the hood
- Format output nicely for humans
- --json for programmatic use
- Show session ID, date, agent, duration, summary

## Acceptance Criteria
- Lists recent sessions
- Shows key metadata (id, date, agent, duration)
- --json works
- Graceful error if CASS not installed

## Dependencies
Requires: Research CASS format task complete

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/session.sh","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:15.177278-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:49:58.843284-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:49:58.843284-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented list_sessions function in session.sh: (1) Shows sessions by agent and top workspaces via CASS stats, (2) --json flag outputs structured JSON, (3) --days/--agent/--limit options for filtering, (4) Graceful error if CASS not installed with install link. Also added check_cass_installed() and get_workspace_sessions() helpers.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-542","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-eli","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:29.699166-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","title":"Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step","description":"# Task: Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step

## Context
Part of EPIC: Pre-Flight Validation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ok)

## Rationale
Users who skip the wizard and run the installer directly should still get pre-flight validation. Currently they only see failures 15 minutes in.

## Proposal
Add preflight as Phase 0 of install.sh:

```bash
run_phase 0 "Pre-Flight Validation" run_preflight_checks
```

### Behavior
- Run all preflight checks
- On FAIL: Show errors, exit (don't waste time)
- On WARN: Show warnings, ask to continue (unless --yes)
- On PASS: Continue silently

### Flag
```bash
--skip-preflight    # Skip pre-flight checks (for debugging)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Preflight runs before Phase 1
- Failures abort immediately with actionable message
- Warnings shown but don't block (with --yes)
- --skip-preflight available for edge cases

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Add Phase 0, add flag
- scripts/preflight.sh: Ensure can be sourced, not just executed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:00.154616-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated preflight into install.sh as Phase 0. Added run_preflight_checks() function, --skip-preflight flag, and auto-download for curl|bash scenario.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0iq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.766175-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","title":"Task: Create command reference page","description":"# Create Command Reference Page

## Parent Feature
Reference Documentation (1y8)

## Goal
Create a searchable command reference page at `/learn/commands` that lists all commands installed by ACFS.

## Design

### Page Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Command Reference                                       │
│                                                         │
│ [🔍 Search commands...]                                 │
│                                                         │
│ Category: [All] [Agents] [Search] [Git] [System]       │
│                                                         │
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ cc                                    [Copy]        │ │
│ │ Claude Code - AI coding assistant                   │ │
│ │ Example: cc "fix the bug in auth.ts"               │ │
│ │ [Full docs →]                                       │ │
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│ │ cod                                   [Copy]        │ │
│ │ Codex CLI - OpenAI coding agent                    │ │
│ │ ...                                                 │ │
│ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│ ...                                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Command Data Structure
```typescript
// lib/commands.ts
interface Command {
  name: string;
  fullName: string;
  description: string;
  category: 'agents' | 'search' | 'git' | 'system' | 'stack';
  example: string;
  docsUrl?: string;
  aliases?: string[];
}

export const COMMANDS: Command[] = [
  // Agents
  { name: 'cc', fullName: 'Claude Code', description: 'AI coding assistant', category: 'agents', example: 'cc "fix the bug"' },
  { name: 'cod', fullName: 'Codex CLI', description: 'OpenAI coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'cod "add tests"' },
  { name: 'gmi', fullName: 'Gemini CLI', description: 'Google coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'gmi "review code"' },
  
  // Search
  { name: 'rg', fullName: 'ripgrep', description: 'Ultra-fast code search', category: 'search', example: 'rg "pattern" ./src' },
  { name: 'fd', fullName: 'fd-find', description: 'Fast file finder', category: 'search', example: 'fd "*.ts"' },
  { name: 'fzf', fullName: 'Fuzzy Finder', description: 'Interactive fuzzy search', category: 'search', example: 'fzf' },
  
  // Git
  { name: 'lazygit', fullName: 'LazyGit', description: 'Visual git interface', category: 'git', example: 'lazygit' },
  { name: 'git', fullName: 'Git', description: 'Version control', category: 'git', example: 'git status' },
  
  // System
  { name: 'ntm', fullName: 'Named Tmux Manager', description: 'Session management', category: 'system', example: 'ntm new project' },
  { name: 'lsd', fullName: 'LSDeluxe', description: 'Modern ls replacement', category: 'system', example: 'lsd -la' },
  { name: 'bat', fullName: 'Bat', description: 'Cat with syntax highlighting', category: 'system', example: 'bat file.ts' },
  { name: 'zoxide', fullName: 'Zoxide', description: 'Smart cd replacement', category: 'system', example: 'z project' },
  
  // Stack
  { name: 'bd', fullName: 'Beads CLI', description: 'Task graph management', category: 'stack', example: 'bd list' },
  { name: 'bv', fullName: 'Beads Viewer', description: 'Kanban task viewer', category: 'stack', example: 'bv' },
  { name: 'ubs', fullName: 'Ultimate Bug Scanner', description: 'Code quality scanner', category: 'stack', example: 'ubs .' },
  { name: 'cass', fullName: 'CASS', description: 'Session search', category: 'stack', example: 'cass' },
  { name: 'cm', fullName: 'CASS Memory', description: 'Procedural memory', category: 'stack', example: 'cm context "auth"' },
  { name: 'caam', fullName: 'CAAM', description: 'Account manager', category: 'stack', example: 'caam status' },
  { name: 'am', fullName: 'Agent Mail', description: 'Agent coordination', category: 'stack', example: 'am status' },
];
```

### Features
1. **Search** - Filter by command name or description
2. **Category tabs** - Quick filter by category
3. **Copy button** - One-click copy command
4. **Expandable** - Show full usage on click
5. **Deep links** - `/learn/commands#cc` jumps to command

### SEO
- Title: "ACFS Command Reference"
- Description: "Quick reference for all commands installed by Agent Flywheel"
- Keywords: individual command names

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Page accessible at `/learn/commands`
- [ ] All ACFS commands listed
- [ ] Search filters results in real-time
- [ ] Category tabs work
- [ ] Copy button copies command
- [ ] Mobile-friendly layout

## File Changes
- Create: `apps/web/lib/commands.ts`
- Create: `apps/web/app/learn/commands/page.tsx`
- Create: `apps/web/components/command-ref-card.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:22:20.811137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented /learn/commands with search, category filters, deep links, and copyable examples.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1y8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.574895-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh)

## Overview
Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment.

## Background & Reasoning
This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must:
- Work when run as root (common VPS default)
- Work when run as ubuntu with sudo
- Be idempotent (safe to run twice)
- Handle connection drops and resume
- Log everything for debugging

## Design Philosophy
**Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction.
**Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work.
**Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs.

## Installer Phases
1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x)
2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers
3. Base packages (apt)
4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins)
5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep)
6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases)
7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail)
8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes
- [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything
- [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified
- [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green
- [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log

## Considerations
- Always prefer upstream installers when available
- Verify each tool after install
- Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions
- Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","close_reason":"All 12 sub-tasks completed. VPS installer fully modularized with libraries for: logging, OS detection, user normalization, shell setup, CLI tools, language runtimes, coding agents, Dicklesworthstone stack, cloud/database tools, smoke test, and canonical zshrc.","labels":["epic","installer"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library

## Description
Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output.

## Functions

```bash
log_step()    # [1/8] Step description (blue)
log_detail()  # Indented detail (gray)
log_success() # ✔ Success message (green)
log_warn()    # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow)
log_error()   # ✖ Error message (red)
log_debug()   # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1
```

## Requirements
- All output to stderr (keep stdout clean)
- Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors)
- Support --verbose flag (show debug)
- Timestamps in log file
- ANSI color codes with fallback

## Log File
Write all output to:
- `/var/log/acfs/install.log`
- `~/.acfs/logs/install.<timestamp>.log`

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/lib/logging.sh

ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log"
ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}"
ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}"

log_step() {
    local step="$1"
    local msg="$2"
    echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All log functions implemented
- [ ] Colors work in terminal
- [ ] --quiet suppresses output
- [ ] --verbose shows debug
- [ ] Log file written with timestamps
- [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf","title":"EPIC: Per-Phase Error Reporting","description":"# Epic: Per-Phase Error Reporting

## Overview
Replace generic 'installation failed' with structured error reports showing exactly which phase/step failed and how to fix it.

## Problem Statement
When installation fails, users see only 'ACFS installation failed' with no indication of which phase (1-10), what step, what the error was, or how to fix it.

## Business Impact
- Users can self-diagnose (reduces support burden)
- 'Phase 6' is actionable; 'failed' is not
- Most failures have known fixes (network, rate limits, deps)

## Technical Approach
1. Add CURRENT_PHASE, CURRENT_STEP, LAST_ERROR globals
2. Create try_step() wrapper for individual operations
3. Build ERROR_PATTERNS map with known fixes
4. Create report_failure() with structured output
5. Log JSON failure report for programmatic use

## Success Criteria
- Failure reports show phase number, step name, error excerpt
- Suggested fixes shown for common error patterns
- Full log path and resume command included
- JSON structured log for tooling

## Dependencies
- Depends on: EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence (for state tracking)

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Error tracking, try_step wrapper, report_failure
- scripts/lib/errors.sh (new): Error patterns database

## Reference
See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 3","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:40:42.022477-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:03:47.660701-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:03:47.660701-05:00","close_reason":"All child tasks closed: qqo (error_tracking.sh with try_step), 5zm (report.sh with report_failure), vwv (errors.sh with ERROR_PATTERNS), 7q1 (98 try_step calls in install.sh). This work will refactor `install.sh` to source generated installers + manifest_index and will touch selection semantics (`--only/--skip/--print-plan`) and curl|bash bootstrap (single archive).

Please keep this epic’s work compatible with your scope:

- generated scripts stay source-safe (no top-level side effects)","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","title":"FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration","description":"## Parent Epic
agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa (Beginner UX Overhaul v1.0)

## Summary

Add interactive SSH public key prompting to the ACFS installer. When users connect with password,
the installer will prompt them to paste their public key, validate it, install it properly, and
enable key-based authentication for both root and ubuntu users.

## Background & Motivation

Currently, users are expected to paste their SSH public key into the VPS provider's web UI during
server creation. This is problematic because:

1. Each provider has different UI for SSH key management
2. Users often can't find where to paste the key
3. Providers may require keys during creation OR after, inconsistently
4. If done wrong, users get "Permission denied" with no helpful guidance

By moving SSH key installation INTO the installer, we:
- Control the entire user experience
- Provide clear prompts and validation
- Can verify the key works before proceeding
- Support any VPS provider universally

## Technical Design

### New Function: `prompt_ssh_key()`

Location: `scripts/lib/user.sh` (alongside existing user functions)

```bash
prompt_ssh_key() {
    # Skip if already have a valid key
    if [[ -f /root/.ssh/authorized_keys ]] && grep -q "^ssh-" /root/.ssh/authorized_keys; then
        log_detail "SSH key already present in root authorized_keys"
        return 0
    fi

    echo ""
    echo "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
    echo "║  SSH Key Setup                                               ║"
    echo "╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣"
    echo "║  You connected with a password. Let's set up SSH key auth   ║"
    echo "║  so you won't need the password anymore.                    ║"
    echo "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
    echo ""
    echo "Paste your PUBLIC key below. It should look like:"
    echo "  ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIxxxxxxxx... acfs"
    echo ""
    echo "You saved this earlier when you ran ssh-keygen."
    echo "(If you skip this, you'll need to enter password each time)"
    echo ""
    read -r -p "Paste your public key (or press Enter to skip): " pubkey

    if [[ -z "$pubkey" ]]; then
        log_warn "SSH key setup skipped. You'll need password for future connections."
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate key format
    if [[ ! "$pubkey" =~ ^ssh-(ed25519|rsa|ecdsa) ]]; then
        log_error "Invalid key format. Expected: ssh-ed25519 or ssh-rsa..."
        log_detail "Make sure you're pasting the PUBLIC key (.pub file)"
        return 1
    fi

    # Install key for root
    mkdir -p /root/.ssh
    echo "$pubkey" >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
    chmod 700 /root/.ssh
    chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
    
    log_success "SSH key installed for root user"
    
    # The existing migrate_ssh_keys() in normalize_user() will copy to ubuntu
    return 0
}
```

### Integration Point

Call `prompt_ssh_key()` at the START of the installer, BEFORE `normalize_user()`:

```bash
# In install.sh main flow
if [[ $EUID -eq 0 ]]; then
    prompt_ssh_key  # New: prompt for key if needed
fi
source_lib "user.sh"
normalize_user  # Existing: includes migrate_ssh_keys()
```

### Validation & Testing

1. **Key format validation**: Must start with `ssh-ed25519`, `ssh-rsa`, or `ssh-ecdsa`
2. **Idempotency**: If key already exists, skip prompting
3. **Skip option**: User can press Enter to skip (uses password)
4. **Migration works**: Verify migrate_ssh_keys() copies to ubuntu

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Installer prompts for SSH key when running as root with no existing key
- [ ] Valid key is installed to /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
- [ ] Invalid key format shows helpful error message
- [ ] User can skip by pressing Enter
- [ ] Key is migrated to ubuntu user by existing migrate_ssh_keys()
- [ ] Subsequent installer runs don't re-prompt (idempotent)
- [ ] Works when piped from curl (stdin handling)

## Edge Cases

1. **Key already exists**: Skip prompt, log that key was found
2. **Invalid key format**: Show error, offer retry
3. **Empty input**: Skip setup, warn about password requirement
4. **Stdin is not a terminal**: Non-interactive mode, skip prompt with warning
5. **Permission issues**: Handle read-only filesystem gracefully

## Testing Plan

1. Fresh VPS with password-only access → should prompt
2. VPS with existing SSH key → should skip
3. Run installer twice → should not re-prompt
4. Paste invalid key → should show helpful error
5. Test on Ubuntu 24.04 and 25.10

## Dependencies

None - this is the foundational change that enables password-first flow.

## Blocks

- Wizard page updates (need installer changes first)
- Post-installation key verification step","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:16.377793-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:29:55.81164-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:29:55.81164-05:00","close_reason":"Feature specification is complete. Technical design for prompt_ssh_key() is fully documented including function signature, edge cases, and integration points. Closing to unblock implementation tasks.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:15:53.643189-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hao","title":"[codex.3] Document OpenAI account types distinction","description":"## Goal
Add clear documentation explaining the difference between OpenAI API accounts and ChatGPT accounts.

## Why This Matters
Many users are confused because OpenAI has TWO distinct account/auth systems:

### 1. OpenAI API Account
- **Purpose**: Programmatic API access (for developers building apps)
- **Auth**: OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
- **Billing**: Pay-per-token, separate API billing
- **Where**: platform.openai.com
- **What uses it**: OpenAI SDK, custom applications
- **NOT used by**: codex-cli

### 2. ChatGPT Account (Pro/Plus/Teams)
- **Purpose**: Consumer chat interface and coding tools
- **Auth**: OAuth via web browser (email/password or SSO)
- **Billing**: Monthly subscription
- **Where**: chat.openai.com
- **What uses it**: ChatGPT website, codex-cli, mobile app

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hby","title":"Implement AI agent updates (claude, codex, gemini)","description":"## What
Implement the 'agents' category for acfs-update:
- Claude Code: claude update (built-in) or reinstall via official installer
- Codex CLI: bun update -g @openai/codex
- Gemini CLI: bun update -g @google/gemini-cli

## Technical Details
- Claude Code has native update: ~/.local/bin/claude update
- If claude update fails, fall back to reinstall via installer
- Codex/Gemini are bun global packages - simple bun update

## Considerations
- Claude update may require re-authentication after major updates
- Codex/Gemini updates are quick (just npm package updates)
- Must use target user's bun, not root's

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All agents update correctly
- [ ] Fallback to reinstall works for Claude
- [ ] Version changes logged
- [ ] Authentication state preserved where possible","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:12.067763-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:14:53.146714-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:14:53.146714-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented version tracking for all agents, Claude update fallback to reinstall","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hby","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.187073-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
